name: Arsenic water sensor (ArsR + CRISPRi)
shortDescription: Detects arsenite in drinking water. The validated ArsR/Pars sensing module gates an sgRNA that, with dCas9, represses a constitutive sfGFP reporter (CRISPRi inverter). Built in safe E. coli K-12.

input.operatingRange: ArsR sensing module responds around the WHO limit of 10 µg/L (~0.13 µM) up to tens of µg/L

sensing.sensorMechanism: Arsenite binds the ArsR repressor, releasing it from the Pars operator and de-repressing the Pars promoter.
sensing.signalFlow: As(III) -> ArsR releases Pars -> Pars transcribes an sgRNA -> sgRNA + dCas9 bind and repress the constitutive sfGFP cassette (CRISPRi) -> green fluorescence falls as arsenic rises (NOT gate). A second CRISPRi inverter stage flips this to signal-ON if a turn-on readout is preferred.

parts.name: ArsR repressor
parts.source: E. coli ars operon; Stocker et al. 2003; iGEM Edinburgh arsenic biosensor

parts.name: Anti-sfGFP sgRNA
parts.source: designed to target the sfGFP promoter region
parts.notes: Transcribed from Pars; spacer targets the reporter promoter for CRISPRi knockdown.

parts.name: sgRNA scaffold (SpCas9)
parts.source: Standard S. pyogenes sgRNA scaffold (Qi et al. 2013)

parts.name: dCas9
parts.source: Addgene dCas9 (D10A/H840A); Qi et al. 2013, Cell
parts.notes: Constitutively expressed; catalytically dead, blocks transcription when guided.

parts.name: J23119 constitutive promoter
parts.notes: Drives the sfGFP reporter (target of CRISPRi).

output.readout: Green fluorescence (plate reader or field fluorimeter)
output.result: Fluorescence change proportional to arsenic concentration.

performance.limitOfDetection: ArsR sensing module is validated near 10 µg/L (WHO arsenic limit).
performance.dynamicRange: ~10-100 µg/L for the native ArsR module
performance.notes: The ArsR/Pars sensing module and CRISPRi are each individually validated in the literature; this specific integrated CRISPRi device is a design template and has not been validated end to end here.

safety.biocontainment: Lab/contained use; auxotrophic kill-switch recommended before any field deployment.
safety.notes: E. coli K-12 is a non-pathogenic BSL-1 host.

buildSteps.title: Design the anti-reporter sgRNA
buildSteps.description: Pick a 20-nt spacer targeting the sfGFP promoter/5'UTR with a PAM; check off-targets against the host genome.

buildSteps.title: Assemble the two transcription units
buildSteps.description: TU1: Pars -> sgRNA. TU2: constitutive dCas9 + J23119-sfGFP reporter. Clone into a SEVA vector.

buildSteps.title: Transform E. coli K-12
buildSteps.description: Transform and select; verify dCas9 and reporter expression in the absence of arsenic.

buildSteps.title: Expose to sample
buildSteps.description: Incubate cells with the water sample (and an arsenic standard curve).

buildSteps.title: Measure fluorescence
buildSteps.description: Read sfGFP; interpolate arsenic concentration from the standard curve.

provenance.source: Design template combining a validated ArsR/Pars arsenic-sensing module with a dCas9 CRISPRi reporter circuit
partsSources: Stocker et al. 2003, Environ. Sci. Technol. (ArsR-based arsenic biosensor)
provenance.license: Parts per their original sources; design template CC BY 4.0
